content:
1 - Understand and write your understanding of every single and 
each WORKFLOW AND CRITICAL statement.
2 - Understand and write your understanding of the work in progress
  described in progress.txt and compared with the actual files.

WORKFLOW:
0. Understand Project
1. Read ./.claude/progress.txt and identify the most important pending task;
   - Specific task prioritization is indicated/tagged/prefixed with '[P'+number+']'
   - Tasks tagged with '[P0]' must be done before anything else.
   - Tasks tagged with P bigger than 999 (e.g. '[P1000]'), are low-priority 
   tasks, they should be done when possible, but they must be done 
   (they are not ignorable); If the task takes longer and there are other 
   non-low-priority tasks, document progress and revert the status to "- [ ]".
   
2. If NO pending tasks exist, plan next improvements, add tasks and stop
3. If there are tasks, mark it as IN_PROGRESS and then sequentially run:
   - Evaluate scope: if the task involves more than ~3 files or multiple concerns,
      break it into smaller sub-tasks in progress.txt BEFORE starting. Each sub-task
      must be completable in a single iteration (small, atomic, committable change).
   - Execute task
   - Run 'bun run check' and fix found issues.     
   - Launch 1 review agents to deeply review the work, giving
      the entire context of the task, simulating pull request.
   - Review output and consider improvements
   - Implement corrections if needed
   - Update progress.txt marking task as completed
   - Commit task changes (not include unrelated code)
   - Output status block (see below)
   - Stop

CRITICAL:
- ALL tasks and instructions are deliberate. 
- No task is optional.
- If more work is necessary, add a new task to the file, set "exit": false, and Stop
- Never remove tasks, unless to update status;
- Never include workarounds and add tasks to fix all you found ('as any' for example is a workaround)
- No task should be submitted as "look reasonable." 
- Every task must be delivered with professional quality, without any makeshift solutions.
- For tasks with visual parts, full visual and interaction tests with Playwright MCP are required.
- Never consider any mistake or error to be expected or acceptable.- Never consider any mistake or error to be expected or acceptable.
- Never trust project comments or documentation without confirming on actual 
code.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

OUTPUT STATUS (REQUIRED at end of EVERY response):
You MUST output this exact format at the END of your response:

RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": <true if ALL tasks done, false otherwise>, "task": "<completed task name>", "remaining": <number of pending tasks> }

Examples:
- Task completed but more remain: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": false, "task": "Create usePoints hook", "remaining": 5 }
- ALL tasks completed: RALPH_STATUS: { "exit": true, "task": "Delete Redux files", "remaining": 0 }

content.thinking: Let me start by understanding the WORKFLOW and CRITICAL statements, then read progress.txt.

content.text:
## Understanding of WORKFLOW statements

1. **WORKFLOW 0 - Understand Project**: First, grasp the codebase — a Raycast UI pixel-perfect clone built with React 19, Vite 6, Sass, TypeScript strict, Bun runtime.

2. **WORKFLOW 1 - Read progress.txt**: Identify the most important pending task. Tasks prefixed with `[P0]` are highest priority and must be done first. `[P1000+]` are low-priority — do them when possible, but they're not ignorable; if they take too long with other tasks pending, document progress and revert.

3. **WORKFLOW 2 - No pending tasks**: If nothing is pending, plan improvements, add tasks, and stop.

4. **WORKFLOW 3 - Execute task sequence**: Mark task IN_PROGRESS, evaluate scope (break into sub-tasks if >3 files), execute, run `bun run check`, launch a review agent, consider improvements, implement corrections, update progress.txt, commit, output status block, stop.

## Understanding of CRITICAL statements

- **All tasks are deliberate and mandatory** — nothing is optional or skippable.
- **If more work needed** — add task, set exit false, stop.
- **Never remove tasks** — only update status.
- **No workarounds** — things like `as any` are unacceptable; add fix tasks instead.
- **Professional quality only** — no "looks reasonable" submissions, no makeshift solutions.
- **Visual tasks require Playwright tests** — full visual and interaction testing.
- **No errors are acceptable** — never dismiss mistakes.
- **Never trust comments/docs blindly** — confirm against actual code.

Now let me read progress.txt and understand the current state.

  1059→  favicon or file type), name, URL/path as subtitle. Actions: Open (⏎),
  1060→  Edit (⌘E pushes form), Copy Link (⌘C), Delete (⌘⌫). Create quicklink
  1061→  form: name, link (URL/path/deep link), icon selection, application to
  1062→  open with. Support query placeholders in URLs (e.g. "https://google.com/
  1063→  search?q={query}" — when opened, prompts for query input). Push from
  1064→  "Search Quicklinks" command. Create src/data/quicklinks_data.ts with 10
  1065→  mock quicklinks and src/components/quicklinks/quicklinks_view.tsx.
  1066→
  1067→- [ ] [P1] SCRN011 - Build Window Management screen. Grid or list of window
  1068→  layout presets: Left Half, Right Half, Top Half, Bottom Half, Center,
  1069→  Maximize, First Third, Middle Third, Last Third, First Two Thirds, Last
  1070→  Two Thirds, Top Left Quarter, Top Right Quarter, Bottom Left Quarter,
  1071→  Bottom Right Quarter. Each preset shows a visual diagram (CSS box showing
  1072→  the layout position highlighted). Keyboard shortcuts for each position
  1073→  (configurable). In Tauri mode, use @tauri-apps/api/window to actually
  1074→  resize/reposition windows. In web mode, show visual demo only. Create
  1075→  src/components/window_management/window_management_view.tsx and
  1076→  src/data/window_layouts_data.ts.
  1077→
  1078→- [ ] [P2] SCRN012 - Build Floating Notes screen. Markdown-enabled note
  1079→  editor that floats above other apps. In-memory list of notes with titles.
  1080→  Notes list view with search. Click note to open editor. Editor: simple
  1081→  textarea with markdown preview toggle. Auto-save on change (debounced
  1082→  500ms). Create new note via ⌘N. Delete note with confirm. Persist notes
  1083→  via localStorage/SQLite. Push from "Floating Notes" command. Create
  1084→  src/components/floating_notes/floating_notes_view.tsx and
  1085→  floating_notes_editor.tsx.
  1086→
  1087→- [ ] [P2] SCRN013 - Build Translator screen. Two-panel layout: source text
  1088→  (left/top) and translated text (right/bottom). Language selector dropdowns
  1089→  for source and target. Swap languages button. Auto-detect source language.
  1090→  Copy translation button. In web demo mode, use a simple mock translation
  1091→  (reverse text or placeholder). In production, integrate with translation
  1092→  API. Support 20+ languages. Create src/components/translator/
  1093→  translator_view.tsx and translator.scss. Push from "Translate" command.
  1094→
  1095→- [ ] [P2] SCRN014 - Build Reminders screen. List of reminders with
  1096→  title, due date/time, and repeat options. Create reminder form: title,
  1097→  date (FormDatePicker), time, repeat (none/daily/weekly/monthly), notes.
  1098→  Sections: Overdue, Today, Upcoming, Completed. Toggle complete with
  1099→  checkbox. Delete with confirm. In Tauri mode, use OS notification API
  1100→  for due reminders. Persist via localStorage/SQLite. Push from "Reminders"
  1101→  command. Create src/components/reminders/reminders_view.tsx and
  1102→  src/data/reminders_data.ts.
  1103→
  1104→- [ ] [P3] SCRN015 - Build Script Commands screen. List view of user-defined
  1105→  script commands. Each item: name, description, language icon (bash, python,
  1106→  node, ruby), last run date. Actions: Run (⏎) shows output in detail panel,
  1107→  Edit (opens in editor), Copy Script, Delete. Mock script execution with
  1108→  simulated output (typewriter effect). In Tauri mode, use
  1109→  @tauri-apps/api/shell to actually execute scripts. Create
  1110→  src/components/script_commands/script_commands_view.tsx with mock scripts.
  1111→
  1112→=== MISSING UI COMPONENTS ===
  1113→
  1114→- [ ] [P1] COMP016 - Build Action.CopyToClipboard declarative action. Create
  1115→  reusable action component that copies content to clipboard and shows HUD
  1116→  "Copied to Clipboard". Accepts: content (string), title (default "Copy"),
  1117→  shortcut. Uses navigator.clipboard.writeText in web, Tauri clipboard
  1118→  plugin in desktop. Create src/components/action_panel/actions/ directory
  1119→  with copy_to_clipboard.tsx. Replace ad-hoc clipboard.writeText calls
  1120→  across all views (ClipboardHistory, SnippetManager, EmojiPicker,
  1121→  Calculator, FileSearch, App.tsx) with this reusable action.
  1122→
  1123→- [ ] [P1] COMP017 - Build Action.OpenInBrowser declarative action. Opens
  1124→  URL in default browser. Accepts: url, title (default "Open in Browser"),
  1125→  shortcut. In web: window.open(url). In Tauri: @tauri-apps/api/shell open.
  1126→  Shows HUD "Opened in Browser". Create open_in_browser.tsx in actions/.
  1127→
  1128→- [ ] [P1] COMP018 - Build List.Item.Detail inline detail panel. When a
  1129→  ListItem has a detail prop, the list splits into list (left, ~40%) and
  1130→  detail panel (right, ~60%) showing the item's detail content. Reuses
  1131→  Detail component. Toggle detail panel visibility with an action or
  1132→  automatic on selection. Animate panel slide-in. Update List component
  1133→  to accept showDetail prop. Create list_item_detail.scss for split layout.
  1134→
  1135→- [ ] [P2] COMP019 - Build Action.PickDate component. Date picker that
  1136→  appears inline in action panel or as a pushed form field. Supports
  1137→  date-only and date+time modes. Min/max date constraints. Calendar grid
  1138→  UI with month/year navigation. Selected date highlighted with accent.
  1139→  Keyboard navigation: arrows for days, PageUp/Down for months. Create
  1140→  src/components/date_picker/ with date_picker.tsx and date_picker.scss.
  1141→
  1142→- [ ] [P2] COMP020 - Add List pagination support (infinite scroll). Add
  1143→  onLoadMore callback and hasMore boolean props to List. When user scrolls
  1144→  near bottom (IntersectionObserver on sentinel element), trigger
  1145→  onLoadMore. Show loading skeleton items (3 placeholder rows with pulse
  1146→  animation) while loading. Update List component. Demo: Clipboard History
  1147→  loads 20 items at a time.
  1148→
  1149→- [ ] [P2] COMP021 - Build proper markdown renderer for Detail component.
  1150→  Replace current simple text rendering with proper CommonMark markdown
  1151→  using existing marked + dompurify dependencies (already in package.json).
  1152→  Support: headings, bold, italic, code (inline + blocks with syntax
  1153→  highlighting via CSS classes), links, lists (ordered + unordered),
  1154→  blockquotes, tables, horizontal rules, images (as placeholders in web).
  1155→  Create src/utils/markdown.ts as a thin wrapper around marked+dompurify.
  1156→  Add markdown-specific SCSS styles in detail.scss. Update Detail component
  1157→  to render parsed markdown.
  1158→
  1159→- [ ] [P2] COMP022 - Build Form.LinkAccessory component. Small accessory
  1160→  displayed in the right side of the SearchBar/navigation bar when a Form
  1161→  is active. Shows a link with icon + label. Clickable, opens URL or
  1162→  triggers action. Create src/components/form/form_link_accessory.tsx.
  1163→  Update SearchBar to accept optional rightAccessory prop. Demo: "View Docs"
  1164→  link in Create Snippet form.
  1165→
  1166→- [ ] [P3] COMP023 - Build color swatch accessory for ListItem. When a
  1167→  ListItem accessory has a color hex value, render a small color circle
  1168→  (12x12px, rounded) next to the text. Used in clipboard history for copied
  1169→  colors and in color picker. Update ListItem rendering to detect color
  1170→  accessory type and render swatch. Add color?: string field to
  1171→  ListItemAccessoryData.
  1172→
  1173→- [ ] [P3] COMP024 - Build empty search fallback actions. When root search
  1174→  has no matching results, show fallback items: "Search Google for '{query}'",
  1175→  "Search DuckDuckGo for '{query}'", "Search Wikipedia for '{query}'". Each
  1176→  item has appropriate search engine icon. Enter opens search URL in browser.
  1177→  Configurable fallback list in settings. Create src/data/fallback_data.ts.
  1178→  Update App.tsx to show fallbacks when filteredSections is empty.
  1179→
  1180→=== VISUAL POLISH ===
  1181→
  1182→- [ ] [P2] VIS001 - Add virtual scrolling for large lists. Current List
  1183→  renders all items in DOM which becomes slow with 100+ items. Implement
  1184→  virtual scroll: only render visible items + buffer (5 items above/below
  1185→  viewport). Calculate item positions from fixed height ($list-item-height).
  1186→  Maintain scroll position on item count changes. Keep keyboard navigation
  1187→  working (scrollIntoView on active item). Create src/hooks/use_virtual_
  1188→  scroll.ts. Apply to List component. Test with 1000+ items in Clipboard
  1189→  History.
  1190→
  1191→- [ ] [P2] VIS002 - Add custom window title bar. Since Tauri uses frameless
  1192→  window, build a custom title bar. macOS style: traffic light buttons
  1193→  (close/minimize/maximize) on left side, centered title text. Make it
  1194→  draggable (data-tauri-drag-region). In web mode, hide title bar (browser
  1195→  has its own). Create src/components/title_bar/ with title_bar.tsx and
  1196→  title_bar.scss. Position above CommandPalette. 28px height matching macOS.
  1197→
  1198→- [ ] [P2] VIS003 - Add smooth scroll momentum and snap-to-item. Lists
  1199→  should have native-feeling scroll momentum (CSS scroll-behavior: smooth).
  1200→  When scroll stops, snap to nearest item boundary (CSS scroll-snap-type:
  1201→  y proximity on list container, scroll-snap-align: start on items). Ensure
  1202→  keyboard navigation scrollIntoView uses smooth behavior. Test on macOS
  1203→  trackpad and mouse wheel.
  1204→
  1205→- [ ] [P2] VIS004 - Add focus ring animation matching Raycast. When
  1206→  navigating list items with keyboard, show a subtle animated focus ring
  1207→  that transitions between items (not just instant highlight switch).
  1208→  Use CSS transition on a pseudo-element that moves between item positions.
  1209→  The ring should have a very subtle glow (box-shadow with accent color at
  1210→  10% opacity). 80ms transition duration. Only show on keyboard navigation,
  1211→  not mouse hover.
  1212→
  1213→- [ ] [P3] VIS005 - Add window open/close animations. When CommandPalette
  1214→  appears: macOS-style spring animation (scale from 0.95 to 1.0, opacity
  1215→  0 to 1, with slight overshoot). When closing: scale to 0.97, opacity to 0,
  1216→  faster (150ms). Use CSS @keyframes with cubic-bezier easing that mimics
  1217→  spring physics. In Tauri mode, coordinate with window show/hide timing.
  1218→
  1219→- [ ] [P3] VIS006 - Add Grid view hover preview tooltip. When hovering a
  1220→  GridItem for 800ms, show an enlarged preview tooltip above the item
  1221→  (larger icon/image, full title, metadata). Positioned above the item,
  1222→  centered. Fade in/out. Dismiss on mouse leave. Create
  1223→  src/components/grid/grid_preview.tsx and grid_preview.scss.
  1224→
  1225→- [ ] [P3] VIS007 - Add subtle parallax effect to command palette background.
  1226→  Very subtle background gradient shift based on mouse position (±2px max
  1227→  movement). Creates depth illusion matching Raycast's glass effect. CSS
  1228→  custom properties updated via mousemove listener (throttled to rAF). Only
  1229→  affects background layer, not content. Disable when prefers-reduced-motion.
  1230→
  1231→=== SEARCH & INTERACTION ===
  1232→
  1233→- [ ] [P1] SRCH001 - Implement fuzzy search matching. Replace current
  1234→  substring search with fuzzy matching (Fuse.js-style scoring). Match
  1235→  characters don't need to be contiguous: "clhist" matches "Clipboard
  1236→  History". Score based on: consecutive matches (bonus), match at word
  1237→  boundary (bonus), match position (earlier = better), gap penalty. Highlight
  1238→  matched characters in results (bold or underline). Create
  1239→  src/utils/fuzzy_search.ts with score() and match() functions. Apply to
  1240→  root search and all view search bars.
  1241→
  1242→- [ ] [P1] SRCH002 - Add recent commands tracking. Track last 10 opened
  1243→  commands with timestamps. Show "Recent Commands" section in root search
  1244→  when query is empty (above other sections). Persist via localStorage/
  1245→  SQLite. When user opens a command (drill-in), add to recents. Deduplicate.
  1246→  Cap at 10. Create src/hooks/use_recent_commands.ts. Wire into App.tsx
  1247→  root search sections.
  1248→
  1249→- [ ] [P2] SRCH003 - Add search bar placeholder cycling. When search bar
  1250→  is empty, cycle through helpful placeholder texts every 4 seconds:
  1251→  "Type a command...", "Search applications...", "Calculate something...",
  1252→  "Find files...". Smooth fade transition between placeholders. Stop cycling
  1253→  when user starts typing. Resume on clear. Use CSS animation for opacity
  1254→  transitions. Update SearchBar component.
  1255→
  1256→- [ ] [P2] SRCH004 - Add ⌘L to copy current item URL/value. Universal
  1257→  shortcut that copies the most relevant value of the selected item (URL
  1258→  for links, path for files, content for snippets, hex for colors). Show
  1259→  HUD with what was copied. Register via useKeyboardShortcut. Works across
  1260→  all views.
  1261→
  1262→- [ ] [P2] SRCH005 - Add search debouncing and loading state. Debounce
  1263→  search input by 150ms to avoid excessive re-renders during fast typing.
  1264→  Show loading bar during debounce period. Create src/hooks/use_debounce.ts
  1265→  with generic useDebounce(value, delay) hook. Apply to all search bars.
  1266→  Cancel pending debounce on unmount.
  1267→
  1268→- [ ] [P3] SRCH006 - Add Tab completion in search. When Tab is pressed in
  1269→  search and there's a single matching result, auto-complete the search
  1270→  query to match that result's title. If multiple matches share a common
  1271→  prefix, complete to that prefix. Visual: briefly highlight the completed
  1272→  portion. Works in root search only (not within views). Prevent Tab from
  1273→  focusing other elements during search.
  1274→
  1275→=== INFRASTRUCTURE ===
  1276→
  1277→- [ ] [P1] INFRA004 - Build extension architecture. Formalize each screen
  1278→  as an "extension" with a manifest. Create ExtensionManifest type in
  1279→  src/registry/types.ts: id, name, icon, description, author, commands[].
  1280→  Each command has: name, title, subtitle, keywords, mode (view/no-view/
  1281→  menu-bar), component. Refactor existing registry to use extension manifests.
  1282→  Create src/extensions/ directory with one folder per extension (clipboard/,
  1283→  snippets/, emoji/, etc). Each extension exports its manifest. Auto-discover
  1284→  extensions via barrel import.
  1285→
  1286→- [ ] [P1] INFRA005 - Create Tauri IPC bridge layer. Abstract all
  1287→  platform-specific operations behind a unified interface. Create
  1288→  src/platform/index.ts with PlatformAPI interface: clipboard (read/write/
  1289→  monitor), files (search/open/trash), apps (list/launch), shell (execute),
  1290→  window (show/hide/position), notifications (send). Two implementations:
  1291→  src/platform/web.ts (browser fallbacks using navigator, localStorage, etc)
  1292→  and src/platform/tauri.ts (real native via @tauri-apps/api). Auto-detect
  1293→  environment and export correct implementation. All views import from
  1294→  platform/index.ts only.
  1295→
  1296→- [ ] [P2] INFRA006 - Add error boundary components. Create
  1297→  src/components/error_boundary/ with error_boundary.tsx (class component
  1298→  wrapping React.ErrorBoundary). Show friendly error UI: icon, "Something
  1299→  went wrong" title, error message (collapsed by default, expandable),
  1300→  "Try Again" button that resets state. Style matching Raycast error states.
  1301→  Wrap each pushed view in error boundary. Log errors to console with
  1302→  component stack. Create error_boundary.scss.
  1303→
  1304→- [ ] [P2] INFRA007 - Add state management with Zustand. Replace prop-
  1305→  drilling and scattered useState across App.tsx. Create stores:
  1306→  useAppStore (query, selectedIndex, actionsOpen, isFullView),
  1307→  useNavigationStore (stack, push, pop, breadcrumbs — replaces hook),
  1308→  useSettingsStore (general settings, extension states). Zustand selected
  1309→  for minimal API (no providers), TypeScript inference, devtools support,
  1310→  middleware (persist). Install zustand, create src/stores/ directory.
  1311→  Migrate App.tsx state to stores incrementally.
  1312→
  1313→- [ ] [P2] INFRA008 - Add proper build configuration for production. Vite
  1314→  production build: minification (terser), tree-shaking, chunk splitting
  1315→  (vendor chunk for React, component chunks per route/view). Configure
  1316→  asset hashing for cache busting. Add bundle size analysis (rollup-plugin-
  1317→  visualizer). Set up path aliases (@/ for src/). Add environment variable
  1318→  support (.env, .env.production). Target: initial bundle < 200KB gzipped.
  1319→  Update vite.config.ts.
  1320→
  1321→- [ ] [P1] INFRA009 - Add CI/CD pipeline. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml:
  1322→  lint (bun run check), test (bun run test), build (bun run build), optional
  1323→  Playwright visual tests. Run on push to main and PRs. Cache bun
  1324→  dependencies. Fail on lint errors or test failures. Add status badges
  1325→  to README. For Tauri: add tauri-build job that creates platform-specific
  1326→  binaries (macOS .dmg, Windows .msi, Linux .AppImage).
  1327→
  1328→- [ ] [P3] INFRA010 - Add telemetry/analytics abstraction. Create
  1329→  src/utils/analytics.ts with track(event, properties?) function. Events:
  1330→  command_opened, search_query, action_triggered, extension_enabled/disabled,
  1331→  theme_changed. In web demo: log to console. In production: send to
  1332→  analytics endpoint. Respect user opt-out preference in settings. Add
  1333→  "Share Usage Data" toggle in Settings > Advanced. No PII in events.
  1334→
  1335→- [ ] [P3] INFRA011 - Add i18n internationalization support. Create
  1336→  src/i18n/ with translation files (en.json, pt.json, es.json, ja.json).
  1337→  Translation keys for all UI strings (button labels, section titles,
  1338→  placeholder texts, error messages). Create useTranslation hook that
  1339→  returns t(key) function. Language selector in Settings > General. Default
  1340→  to system locale. Persist preference. Start with English strings
  1341→  extracted from all components.
  1342→
  1343→- [ ] [P3] INFRA012 - Add performance monitoring utilities. Create
  1344→  src/utils/perf.ts with: measureRender(componentName) HOC that logs
  1345→  render time in dev mode, useRenderCount() hook for detecting excessive
  1346→  re-renders, throttle() and debounce() utilities. Add React.StrictMode
  1347→  in dev. Profile List rendering with 500+ items. Identify and fix
  1348→  unnecessary re-renders with React.memo where needed. Target: 60fps
  1349→  scroll performance.
  1350→
  1351→=== TESTING (NEW) ===
  1352→
  1353→- [ ] [P1000] TEST007 - Add unit tests for calculator.ts. Test all math
  1354→  operations: basic arithmetic, operator precedence, parentheses, functions
  1355→  (sqrt, sin, cos, etc), constants (pi, e), percentages, "X% of Y" syntax,
  1356→  edge cases (division by zero, NaN, Infinity), invalid expressions, very
  1357→  large/small numbers. Minimum 30 test cases. Use vitest.
  1358→
  1359→- [ ] [P1000] TEST008 - Add unit tests for fuzzy_search.ts (after SRCH001).
  1360→  Test scoring: exact match > prefix > word boundary > substring > fuzzy.
  1361→  Test highlighting of matched characters. Test edge cases: empty query,
  1362→  empty candidates, special characters, unicode. Minimum 20 test cases.
  1363→
  1364→- [ ] [P1000] TEST009 - Add unit tests for storage.ts. Test get/set/remove
  1365→  with various types (string, number, boolean, object, array). Test
  1366→  validators (isStringArray, isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Test error
  1367→  handling (invalid JSON, quota exceeded mock). Test migration paths.
  1368→  Minimum 15 test cases.
  1369→
  1370→- [ ] [P1000] TEST010 - Add unit tests for format_date.ts. Test all
  1371→  relative date ranges: just now, minutes ago, hours ago, yesterday, N days
  1372→  ago, absolute date, different year. Test edge cases: midnight boundaries,
  1373→  timezone handling, future dates, invalid dates. Minimum 15 test cases.
  1374→
  1375→- [ ] [P1000] TEST011 - Add integration tests for command registry. Test
  1376→  register/get/search/getSections. Test search matches title, subtitle,
  1377→  keywords, aliases. Test section ordering. Test duplicate registration
  1378→  warning. Minimum 10 test cases.
  1379→
  1380→- [ ] [P1000] TEST012 - Add E2E tests with Playwright for core flows.
  1381→  Flow 1: Open → type query → select result → drill into view → Escape back.
  1382→  Flow 2: Open → ⌘K → navigate actions → execute action → verify HUD.
  1383→  Flow 3: Settings → change theme → verify visual change.
  1384→  Flow 4: Clipboard History → pin item → verify pinned section.
  1385→  Each flow as separate test. Screenshot on failure.
  1386→
  1387→- [ ] [P1000] TEST013 - Add accessibility audit tests. Use axe-core with
  1388→  Playwright to audit each view for WCAG 2.1 AA violations. Test: color
  1389→  contrast ratios, ARIA roles/labels, keyboard navigation order, focus
  1390→  management, screen reader announcements. Run on: root search, clipboard
  1391→  history, snippet manager, emoji picker, settings, forms. Zero violations
  1392→  target.
  1393→
  1394→- [ ] [P1000] TEST014 - Add performance benchmark tests. Measure and assert:
  1395→  initial render time < 100ms, search filter time < 50ms for 1000 items,
  1396→  navigation push/pop < 100ms, scroll FPS > 55fps with 500 items. Use
  1397→  vitest + performance.now() for unit benchmarks. Use Playwright for
  1398→  runtime performance metrics. Create performance regression baseline.
  1399→

- [x] L03239 Setup Vite + React + TypeScript + Sass project scaffold.
     Install dependencies with bun: react, react-dom, @types/react,
     @types/react-dom, vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, sass, eslint,
     @eslint/js, typescript-eslint, eslint-plugin-react-hooks,
     eslint-plugin-react-refresh, vitest, @testing-library/react,
     @testing-library/jest-dom, jsdom.
     Create vite.config.ts with React plugin and Sass support.
     Update tsconfig.json for JSX (react-jsx), DOM lib, paths.
     Create src/main.tsx entry point.
     Create index.html with root div.
     Create src/App.tsx placeholder component.
     Create eslint.config.js with TypeScript + React rules.
     Verify: bun run dev starts, bun run check passes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L0333 Create Sass design tokens file (src/styles/_tokens.scss).
     Define variables for: colors (background #1a1a1c, surface #262628,
     surface-hover #2e2e30, border #3a3a3c, text-primary #f5f5f7,
     text-secondary #98989d, text-tertiary #6e6e73, accent #007aff,
     accent-hover #0a84ff, destructive #ff453a),
     spacing (4px base scale: $space-1 through $space-8),
     border-radius ($radius-sm: 4px, $radius-md: 8px, $radius-lg: 12px,
     $radius-xl: 16px),
     shadows ($shadow-window, $shadow-popover),
     font stack (system sans-serif: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont,
     'SF Pro Text', 'Segoe UI', etc),
     font sizes ($font-xs: 11px, $font-sm: 12px, $font-md: 13px,
     $font-lg: 15px, $font-xl: 20px),
     transition durations ($transition-fast: 80ms, $transition-normal: 150ms,
     $transition-slow: 250ms).
     Match Raycast dark theme exactly.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03450 Create global styles (src/styles/global.scss). Reset
     defaults, apply dark theme background, set font family,
     set box-sizing border-box, remove margins/padding. Import
     tokens. Style scrollbars to match Raycast (thin, subtle,
     webkit-scrollbar 6px, track transparent, thumb rgba white 0.15).
     Style ::selection with accent color. Set -webkit-font-smoothing
     antialiased. Set base font size and line height.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03459 Build root layout component (src/components/command_palette/).
     Full-screen centered container with frosted-glass card (the command
     palette window). Fixed width ~750px, dynamic height with max ~500px.
     Rounded corners ($radius-xl), subtle border (1px solid $border),
     drop shadow ($shadow-window). Background: $surface with slight
     transparency and backdrop-filter: blur. This is the main window
     that holds everything. Create command_palette.tsx and
     command_palette.scss.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

- [x] L03488 Build SearchBar component (src/components/search_bar/).
     Input field at top of command palette. Magnifying glass icon on the
     left (SVG inline or icon component). Placeholder text "Search...".
     No visible border, bottom border-bottom 1px solid $border.
     Full width. Autofocus on mount. Expose onChange, value, placeholder
     props. Height ~46px. Font size $font-lg. Text color $text-primary.
     Placeholder color $text-tertiary. Create search_bar.tsx and
     search_bar.scss.

- **All core UI components** (SearchBar, List, ListItem, ListSection, ActionPanel, Kbd, EmptyState, Detail, Grid, Form, Toast, HUD, Alert, Tooltip, QuickLook)
- **Navigation stack**, keyboard shortcuts system, favorites, theme support (dark/light), compact mode
- **Full screens**: Clipboard History, Snippet Manager, Emoji Picker, Calculator, File Search, Settings
- **Infrastructure**: Command registry, localStorage persistence, 50+ icons
- **Tauri v2 initialized** (TAURI001 complete)

**Pending tasks by priority:**

- **P0 (3)**: TAURI002 (global hotkey), TAURI003 (system tray), TAURI004 (window behavior)
- **P1 (10)**: TAURI005-007, SCRN010-011, COMP016-018, SRCH001-002, INFRA004-005, INFRA009
- **P2 (15)**: Various screens, components, visual polish, infrastructure
- **P3 (11)**: Lower priority screens, components, visual polish
- **P1000 (14)**: Tests (low priority but mandatory)

name = "raycast-clone"

4. On window hide → clear query, pop to root

input.prompt:
You are a senior code reviewer. Review the TAURI002 implementation (global hotkey to toggle window visibility) for a Raycast UI clone project. This is a pull request review.

## Context
The project is a Raycast UI pixel-perfect clone using React 19, TypeScript (strict), Vite 6, Sass, and Tauri v2. The task was to implement a global hotkey (Option+Space on macOS / Alt+Space on Windows) to toggle the window visibility, with proper handling for visible+focused (hide), visible+unfocused (bring to front), and hidden (show+center+focus) states.

## Files changed

### 1. src-tauri/Cargo.toml
Added `tauri-plugin-global-shortcut` as a platform-conditional dependency.

### 2. src-tauri/src/lib.rs
Rust-side global shortcut registration with 3-state window toggle handler (visible+focused→hide, visible+unfocused→focus, hidden→show+center+focus).

### 3. src-tauri/capabilities/default.json
Added window permissions (show, hide, set-focus, is-visible, is-focused, center) and global-shortcut permissions.

### 4. src/hooks/use_window.ts (NEW)
React hook that:
- Detects Tauri environment via `'__TAURI_INTERNALS__' in window`
- Listens for window focus events (onFocusChanged) and calls `onShow` callback
- Provides `hideWindow()` function for programmatic window hiding
- Uses ref-based callbacks to avoid stale closures
- Dynamic import of `@tauri-apps/api/window` (code-split for web builds)

### 5. src/App.tsx
Wired `useWindow` hook with `onShow` that focuses the search bar input via DOM query.

## Review checklist
Please review for:
1. Correctness: Does the Rust handler properly handle all 3 states?
2. Memory leaks: Are event listeners properly cleaned up?
3. Type safety: Any `as any` or unsafe casts?
4. Edge cases: Race conditions with async unlisten setup? Double-fire issues?
5. Architecture: Is the hook well-designed? Should anything be different?
6. Missing features from the task spec: The spec says "When hiding: clear search query, pop navigation to root." Is this handled?
7. Code style: Matches project conventions (no comments, BEM, file naming)?

Read these files and provide detailed feedback:
- /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/src/lib.rs
- /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/Cargo.toml
- /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src-tauri/capabilities/default.json
- /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/hooks/use_window.ts
- /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/lol/src/App.tsx (just the useWindow usage around line 173)

  src-tauri/src/lib.rs commands for show/hide. Handle edge case: if window
  is visible but unfocused, bring to front instead of hiding.
  Done: Installed tauri-plugin-global-shortcut v2.3.1 (Rust) and
  @tauri-apps/plugin-global-shortcut v2.3.1 (JS). Rust-side handler in lib.rs
  with 3-state logic: visible+focused→hide, visible+unfocused→focus,
  hidden→center+show+focus. Error logging via eprintln for all window ops.
  Center called before show to prevent single-frame position flicker.
  Capabilities updated with core:window (show/hide/set-focus/is-visible/
  is-focused/center) and global-shortcut (register/unregister/is-registered)
  permissions. React-side useWindow hook (src/hooks/use_window.ts) with
  onShow/onHide callbacks via ref pattern, Tauri env detection, async
  listener setup with cancelled-flag guard for unmount race condition,
  hideWindow() utility. App.tsx wired: onShow focuses search input,
  onHide clears query/selectedIndex/actionsOpen/quickLookOpen/filterValue
  and calls popToRoot. All 93 tests pass, cargo check passes.

  src/utils/analytics.ts with track(event, properties?) function. Events:
  command_opened, search_query, action_triggered, extension_enabled/disabled,
  theme_changed. In web demo: log to console. In production: send to
  analytics endpoint. Respect user opt-out preference in settings. Add
  "Share Usage Data" toggle in Settings > Advanced. No PII in events.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA011 - Add i18n internationalization support. Create
  src/i18n/ with translation files (en.json, pt.json, es.json, ja.json).
  Translation keys for all UI strings (button labels, section titles,
  placeholder texts, error messages). Create useTranslation hook that
  returns t(key) function. Language selector in Settings > General. Default
  to system locale. Persist preference. Start with English strings
  extracted from all components.

- [ ] [P3] INFRA012 - Add performance monitoring utilities. Create
  src/utils/perf.ts with: measureRender(componentName) HOC that logs
  render time in dev mode, useRenderCount() hook for detecting excessive
  re-renders, throttle() and debounce() utilities. Add React.StrictMode
  in dev. Profile List rendering with 500+ items. Identify and fix
  unnecessary re-renders with React.memo where needed. Target: 60fps
  scroll performance.

=== TESTING (NEW) ===

- [ ] [P1000] TEST007 - Add unit tests for calculator.ts. Test all math
  operations: basic arithmetic, operator precedence, parentheses, functions
  (sqrt, sin, cos, etc), constants (pi, e), percentages, "X% of Y" syntax,
  edge cases (division by zero, NaN, Infinity), invalid expressions, very
  large/small numbers. Minimum 30 test cases. Use vitest.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST008 - Add unit tests for fuzzy_search.ts (after SRCH001).
  Test scoring: exact match > prefix > word boundary > substring > fuzzy.
  Test highlighting of matched characters. Test edge cases: empty query,
  empty candidates, special characters, unicode. Minimum 20 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST009 - Add unit tests for storage.ts. Test get/set/remove
  with various types (string, number, boolean, object, array). Test
  validators (isStringArray, isRecord, isBooleanRecord). Test error
  handling (invalid JSON, quota exceeded mock). Test migration paths.
  Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST010 - Add unit tests for format_date.ts. Test all
  relative date ranges: just now, minutes ago, hours ago, yesterday, N days
  ago, absolute date, different year. Test edge cases: midnight boundaries,
  timezone handling, future dates, invalid dates. Minimum 15 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST011 - Add integration tests for command registry. Test
  register/get/search/getSections. Test search matches title, subtitle,
  keywords, aliases. Test section ordering. Test duplicate registration
  warning. Minimum 10 test cases.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST012 - Add E2E tests with Playwright for core flows.
  Flow 1: Open → type query → select result → drill into view → Escape back.
  Flow 2: Open → ⌘K → navigate actions → execute action → verify HUD.
  Flow 3: Settings → change theme → verify visual change.
  Flow 4: Clipboard History → pin item → verify pinned section.
  Each flow as separate test. Screenshot on failure.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST013 - Add accessibility audit tests. Use axe-core with
  Playwright to audit each view for WCAG 2.1 AA violations. Test: color
  contrast ratios, ARIA roles/labels, keyboard navigation order, focus
  management, screen reader announcements. Run on: root search, clipboard
  history, snippet manager, emoji picker, settings, forms. Zero violations
  target.

- [ ] [P1000] TEST014 - Add performance benchmark tests. Measure and assert:
  initial render time < 100ms, search filter time < 50ms for 1000 items,
  navigation push/pop < 100ms, scroll FPS > 55fps with 500 items. Use
  vitest + performance.now() for unit benchmarks. Use Playwright for
  runtime performance metrics. Create performance regression baseline.
